mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with rate declines.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending earnings weakness, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below 52% in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer and business demand expansion, driving corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce a positive return in > 55% of triggered cases, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression over 5 years.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a confirmed economic expansion reflects strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, creating short-term positive momentum for equity markets over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) when applied outside expansion phases, or 5-day forward returns are negative in > 60% of rising INDPRO observations during expansions.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month CPI increase exceeding 0.4% triggers expectations of aggressive Federal Reserve tightening, which raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in > 60% of high-inflation trigger months, or the inflation threshold fails to separate winning and losing months with statistical significance (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month payroll employment growth reflects a healthy labor market that supports wage growth and consumer spending, directly boosting corporate earnings and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC for PAYEMS changes falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with payroll growth in out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly GDP growth and elevated CPI inflation indicate demand-pull price pressures that force monetary policy tightening, creating stagflationary risks that suppress equity valuations and yield negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: The interaction signal produces positive returns in > 58% of cases, or the combined effect of GDP and inflation shows no incremental predictive power over single-series models in a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5% signals a sudden supply-side contraction or manufacturing slowdown, triggering immediate risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where INDPRO drops > 0.5%, or the threshold signal accuracy degrades below 50% in out-of-sample validation.
